pi_items.source: task.json and embedded dataset definition in install_gretl.sh
Embedded usa.gdt dataset structure with variable name 'gdp'
Embedded usa.gdt metadata shows type='time-series' with frequency='4' (quarterly)

FRED GDPC96 series is measured in billions of chained 2009 dollars. BEA changed the reference year from 2005 to 2009 in Q2 2013 comprehensive revision. The embedded usa.gdt description claims 2005 dollars, but GDPC96 (the cited FRED series) has been in 2009 dollars since 2013. Since POE5 was published in 2018, the actual series used would be in 2009 dollars.

pi_items.verified_value: CPI inflation rate, annualized % (CPIAUCSL from FRED)
pi_items.source: Embedded usa.gdt shows inf variable with label 'CPI inflation rate, annualized % (FRED: CPIAUCSL)'. CPIAUCSL verified as Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers from BLS via FRED.

pi_items.metadata_value: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database, via Hill et al. POE5
pi_items.verified_value: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database (GDPC96 for GDP, CPIAUCSL for inflation), used in Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (Hill, Griffiths, Lim, 2018)
pi_items.source: Embedded usa.gdt description states 'Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database' and 'Used in Principles of Econometrics (Hill, Griffiths, Lim), Chapter 12'. Web search confirmed POE5 published February 21, 2018 by Wiley, authored by R. Carter Hill, William E. Griffiths, and Guay C. Lim.
Embedded usa.gdt shows observations count='103' and contains 103 obs elements with gdp and inf values from 1984Q1 to 2009Q3
Embedded usa.gdt shows variables count='2' with gdp and inf defined

pi_items.metadata_value: real GDP in billions of chained 2005 dollars
pi_items.verified_value: Real Gross Domestic Product (billions of chained 2009 dollars, seasonally adjusted annual rate) from FRED GDPC96
pi_items.source: Embedded usa.gdt variable label states 'Real GDP, billions of chained 2005 dollars (FRED: GDPC96)' but GDPC96 actual units are in chained 2009 dollars since 2013 BEA comprehensive revision

privileged_info_summary: The usa.gdt dataset contains real US quarterly macroeconomic data from 1984Q1 to 2009Q3 (103 observations). It includes two variables: (1) gdp - Real Gross Domestic Product measured in billions of dollars from FRED series GDPC96, and (2) inf - CPI inflation rate (annualized %) from FRED series CPIAUCSL. The data source is the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database, used in Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition by Hill, Griffiths, and Lim (published 2018). DISCREPANCY NOTE: The dataset description claims GDP units are 'billions of chained 2005 dollars,' but GDPC96 has been measured in chained 2009 dollars since BEA's comprehensive revision in Q2 2013. Since POE5 was published in 2018, the actual series used would be in 2009 dollars, not 2005 dollars.
